cambridge.name: University of Cambridge
cambridge.overview: The University of Cambridge is one of the world's oldest and most prestigious universities, founded in 1209. Its Computer Laboratory was the birthplace of modern computing — Alan Turing, Maurice Wilkes (EDSAC), and the ARM architecture all trace back to Cambridge. The Department of Computer Science and Technology is consistently ranked #1 in the UK and top 5 globally.

scholarships: Cambridge Bursary Scheme (up to £3,500/year for low household income)
funding.bursaries: Means-tested up to £3,500/year; ~25% of students receive support
funding.paymentPlan: Termly or by instalment via Cambridge Trust

employability.careersService: Cambridge Careers Service offers dedicated CS sector advisers; CS Mailing List has 200+ employer events per year.

libraries: Betty and Gordon Moore Library (Computer Science)
facilities.sportsFacilities: World-class — Olympic-standard facilities at Wilberforce Road
facilities.labs: Dedicated CS labs with HPC access; quantum computing testbed
facilities.wellbeing: College-based wellbeing officers and University Counselling Service

accommodation.description: All undergraduates are guaranteed accommodation within their college for all three years of the degree.
accommodation.privateRentAreas: N/A — most students live in college throughout

personalStatementTips: Demonstrate sustained engagement with mathematics and programming beyond the syllabus
Mention specific books, courses or projects (Project Euler, competitive programming)
Show clear evidence of self-directed learning
application.typicalTimeline: Apply by 15 October UCAS deadline; Cambridge Online Preliminary Application (COPA) also required; interviews December

highlights: Strong alumni network in tech and finance

cityLife.description: Cambridge is a compact, beautiful, cycle-friendly city. The Silicon Fen tech cluster (ARM, Microsoft Research, AstraZeneca, Apple) sits just outside the city, creating exceptional placement and graduate opportunities.
cityLife.transport: Walkable city; cycle is the norm
cityLife.nightlife: Quieter than London but lively student nightlife

oxford.name: University of Oxford
oxford.overview: The University of Oxford's Department of Computer Science (formerly the Computing Laboratory) was founded in 1957 by Christopher Strachey and is one of the largest CS departments in the UK. Oxford CS combines deep theoretical foundations (logic, semantics, formal methods) with strong applied research in AI, security and quantum computing.

scholarships: Oxford Bursary (up to £5,720/year for lowest-income students)
Crankstart Scholarship for UK students with household income <£32,500
funding.paymentPlan: Annual or termly via University Trust

employability.careersService: Oxford Careers Service runs dedicated tech and finance fairs; strong alumni network.

facilities.studySpaces: College libraries open 24/7; department reading rooms
facilities.labs: Dedicated CS labs; access to HPC and quantum testbed
facilities.wellbeing: College-based welfare teams and University Counselling Service

accommodation.description: Most colleges guarantee accommodation for all three years.

personalStatementTips: Show genuine intellectual curiosity about CS as a discipline (not just programming)
Demonstrate engagement with mathematics — beyond A-level
Mention any reading you've done (Hopcroft & Ullman, SICP, etc.)
application.typicalTimeline: Apply by 15 October UCAS deadline; MAT test in late October; interviews December

cityLife.description: Oxford is a compact, historic city with a thriving CS/AI scene — the Oxford Science Park hosts dozens of spinouts and DeepMind has a major presence in Oxford.
cityLife.transport: Walkable; excellent bus and cycle network
cityLife.nightlife: Lively student pubs and union events

keyFacts: Home of the Oxford Robotics Institute

imperial-college-london.name: Imperial College London
imperial-college-london.overview: Imperial College London is the UK's leading STEM-focused university, located in South Kensington. The Department of Computing was one of the first CS departments in the UK (founded 1964) and is consistently ranked top 3 in the country. Imperial CS produces more startup founders per graduate than any UK CS department.

employability.careersService: Imperial Careers Service runs Big Tech fairs and a dedicated CS jobs board.

facilities.labs: Latest GPU clusters for ML; robotics labs; quantum computing testbed

accommodation.description: First-year halls in South Kensington, Hammersmith or Acton.

personalStatementTips: Demonstrate strong mathematical aptitude (Imperial cares about depth)
Mention research interests if any (helps for MEng)
Show evidence of programming projects
application.typicalTimeline: Apply by 29 January UCAS deadline; TMUA test required

studentLife.sportsTeams: Strong rowing, rugby; Varsity match v UCL

cityLife.description: South Kensington puts you between Hyde Park, Chelsea and Kensington — surrounded by museums (V&A, Natural History, Science Museum). Imperial's central London location is unmatched for tech employer access.
cityLife.transport: Tube — Piccadilly Line and District Line
cityLife.nightlife: Vast London nightlife; student bars at the Union

keyFacts: Highest startup-founder rate of any UK CS dept

university-college-london.name: University College London
university-college-london.overview: University College London (UCL) is a top-10 global university located in central London. The UCL Department of Computer Science is one of the largest in Europe and has world-leading research in AI, HCI, security and software systems. UCL's AI Centre (formerly Centre for AI) is one of the largest concentrations of AI talent in Europe.

employability.careersService: UCL Careers Service runs sector-focused weeks; CS careers fair attracts 80+ employers.

facilities.sportsFacilities: Bloomsbury Fitness, Somers Town playing fields
facilities.labs: Dedicated GPU clusters; HCI research labs; cyber labs
facilities.wellbeing: UCL Student Support and Wellbeing Service

accommodation.description: Halls within walking distance of campus or short tube ride.

personalStatementTips: UCL values breadth — show interest in CS beyond pure programming
Mention any data science or HCI interests if relevant
Strong academic record matters most

highlights: UCL AI Society (one of UK's largest)

cityLife.description: UCL's Bloomsbury location places you at the heart of academic London, walking distance from the British Museum, Russell Square and the West End.
cityLife.transport: Multiple Tube lines within walking distance

keyFacts: UCL AI Centre — largest concentration of AI talent in Europe

edinburgh.name: University of Edinburgh
edinburgh.overview: The University of Edinburgh's School of Informatics is one of the largest and most highly-rated computer science / AI research centres in Europe. Edinburgh established the world's first AI department in 1963 and remains a global leader in machine learning, natural language processing and robotics. The 4-year MA Hons / BSc structure follows the Scottish system.

employability.careersService: Informatics has a dedicated careers officer and runs an annual Industry Day with 40+ employers.

facilities.labs: GPU clusters; robotics labs; HCI labs

accommodation.description: First-year guaranteed across multiple halls in the city centre and around Pollock estate.

personalStatementTips: Edinburgh values curiosity about AI — mention if relevant
4-year MA Hons emphasises breadth in Year 1
Show evidence of mathematical engagement
application.typicalTimeline: Apply by 29 January UCAS deadline; earlier deadline for joint degrees

highlights: Edinburgh University Hoppers Society (women in CS)

cityLife.description: Edinburgh is consistently rated one of the best UK cities for students — beautiful architecture, lively festivals, vibrant tech scene (Skyscanner, FanDuel, Rockstar North all HQ'd here).
cityLife.nightlife: Lively student scene; Edinburgh Fringe in August

keyFacts: Bayes Centre — Scotland's data science hub

manchester.name: University of Manchester
manchester.overview: The University of Manchester traces computer science back to 1948 when the Manchester Baby ran the world's first stored-program computer instruction. The Department of Computer Science is one of the largest in the UK, with strong research in AI, advanced computer architecture and software engineering. Manchester is a major tech hub with employers including IBM, Bloomberg and the BBC.

employability.careersService: CS-specific careers fair in October; integrated industrial experience scheme.

facilities.sportsFacilities: Sugden Sports Centre, Armitage Sports Centre
facilities.labs: Dedicated CS labs; HPC access via N8

accommodation.description: Manchester guarantees accommodation to all first-year undergraduates.

personalStatementTips: Demonstrate enthusiasm for both theory and applications
Mention any coding projects on GitHub
Show awareness of the discipline beyond A-level

studentLife.union: Manchester Students' Union (one of largest in UK)

cityLife.description: Manchester is a vibrant, cosmopolitan city with a thriving tech scene (Manchester Tech City), excellent music venues, and one of the most affordable major UK cities for students.
cityLife.nightlife: Iconic UK nightlife — Northern Quarter, Deansgate

notableAlumni: Alan Turing (worked here on Manchester Baby)
keyFacts: Largest CS school in UK by undergraduate intake

bristol.name: University of Bristol
bristol.overview: The University of Bristol's School of Computer Science is renowned for its research in AI, computer vision and quantum computing. Bristol is a major UK tech hub — Aardman Animations, BBC Natural History Unit, Graphcore (AI chip startup), and Dyson all have major presence in the city.

employability.careersService: Strong relationships with Bristol's local tech ecosystem.

accommodation.description: First-year accommodation guaranteed if applied by deadline.

personalStatementTips: Mention any interest in vision/graphics — Bristol has strong heritage
Show evidence of independent learning
A* in Maths is essential

cityLife.description: Bristol is a creative, sustainable city with one of the UK's strongest tech and animation scenes. The harbourside, Stokes Croft and Clifton make for vibrant student living.

warwick.name: University of Warwick
warwick.overview: The University of Warwick's Department of Computer Science is highly regarded for its theoretical computer science, algorithms research, and applied AI. Warwick consistently produces graduates for top tech firms and financial institutions, with strong links to Jane Street, Citadel and JPMorgan.

employability.careersService: Dedicated Warwick CS careers service runs quant fairs and tech employer days.

facilities.labs: Modern labs with full GPU access
facilities.wellbeing: Wellbeing Services and dedicated CS welfare tutor

personalStatementTips: Show enthusiasm for the mathematical side of CS
Warwick values evidence of breadth — competitions, MOOCs, projects
A* in Maths essential

highlights: Warwick Finance Societies (close to Quant Fund)

cityLife.description: Warwick's self-contained campus is between Coventry, Leamington Spa and Kenilworth — most students live in lively Leamington in 2nd/3rd year.
cityLife.transport: Free Stagecoach buses to Leamington and Coventry
cityLife.nightlife: On-campus venues; nights out in Leamington and Coventry

keyFacts: Discrete Maths joint degree among UK's most selective

southampton.name: University of Southampton
southampton.overview: The University of Southampton's School of Electronics and Computer Science (ECS) is one of the largest in Europe and a recognised UK leader in cyber security (NCSC Academic Centre of Excellence) and web science. The Web Foundation was founded by Sir Tim Berners-Lee here.

employability.careersService: ECS has dedicated careers officer; strong defence and tech employer links.

personalStatementTips: Southampton values evidence of independent project work
If applying for Cyber Security, mention CTF/Hack The Box if relevant
Maths A-level required

cityLife.description: Southampton is a port city with a young, energetic vibe and easy access to the New Forest and south coast beaches.
cityLife.nightlife: Lively Bedford Place and Oxford Street student scene

keyFacts: NCSC Academic Centre of Excellence in Cyber Security
One of Europe's largest ECS schools

bath.name: University of Bath
bath.overview: The University of Bath is renowned for its exceptional graduate employability — consistently top 5 UK universities for graduate outcomes. The Department of Computer Science is one of the strongest in the UK for industrial placement, with 80%+ of students taking a paid 12-month placement that materially boosts career outcomes.

employability.careersService: Bath's placement programme is one of UK's most established (Bath was a 'sandwich' pioneer).

facilities.sportsFacilities: Sports Training Village (Team GB training venue)
facilities.labs: Modern labs with placement-year industry partnerships

accommodation.description: First-year accommodation guaranteed on campus or in city.
accommodation.privateRentAreas: Oldfield Park, Bear Flat, Combe Down

personalStatementTips: Mention placement-year interest if applying for the sandwich course
Bath values clear evidence of self-direction
A* in Maths required

cityLife.description: Bath is a stunning UNESCO World Heritage city — Georgian architecture, Roman baths, and great access to Bristol and the Cotswolds.
cityLife.transport: Compact walkable city; bus to campus
cityLife.nightlife: Smaller student scene than Bristol, but lively student venues

keyFacts: One of UK's best CS placement programmes

glasgow.name: University of Glasgow
glasgow.overview: The University of Glasgow's School of Computing Science is one of the largest in the UK and a leader in systems research. Glasgow's connection to Scotland's expanding tech scene — including Skyscanner, FanDuel and significant fintech — provides excellent graduate opportunities.

employability.careersService: Strong links with Glasgow's expanding fintech sector.

libraries: Glasgow University Library (one of UK's largest)

personalStatementTips: Scottish system: 4-year MSci preferred
Show evidence of mathematical engagement
Maths required at A

studentLife.union: Glasgow University Union (one of oldest in world)

cityLife.description: Glasgow has a buzzing music, arts and food scene with very affordable living. The fintech sector is growing rapidly (Glasgow has the UK's second-largest fintech cluster after London).
cityLife.nightlife: Some of UK's best music venues

keyFacts: Founded 1451 — one of UK's oldest universities
Largest Computing Science school in Scotland

birmingham.name: University of Birmingham
birmingham.overview: The University of Birmingham's School of Computer Science has a strong reputation in theoretical computer science, machine learning and security. The university's location in the UK's second city provides excellent links to Goldman Sachs, HSBC, PwC and Deutsche Bank's Birmingham operations.

employability.careersService: Strong links with Birmingham's financial services hub.

personalStatementTips: Birmingham values evidence of curiosity beyond A-level syllabus
Maths A required

cityLife.description: Birmingham is the UK's second city with one of Europe's youngest populations and a £1 billion+ city centre regeneration. Tech employer presence is growing fast.

keyFacts: Strong banking and finance employer presence

leeds.name: University of Leeds
leeds.overview: The University of Leeds' School of Computer Science is one of the largest in the UK with strong research in AI, data analytics and human-machine systems. Leeds is the financial and digital capital of the North, providing excellent graduate opportunities.

employability.careersService: Strong links with Yorkshire tech employers and London via the LBS pipeline.

facilities.labs: Dedicated GPU labs; HCI usability labs

accommodation.description: First-year accommodation guaranteed if applied by deadline.

personalStatementTips: Maths A-level required
Show interest in CS as a discipline

studentLife.union: Leeds University Union (UK's best, NUS award)

cityLife.description: Leeds is the financial capital of the North with one of the UK's best student scenes — major nightlife, music, sport (Headingley cricket).

durham.name: University of Durham
durham.overview: Durham University's Department of Computer Science is small, selective and highly research-focused. Durham consistently ranks in the UK's top 10 for graduate outcomes and provides a collegiate experience similar to Oxford and Cambridge, in a beautiful medieval city.

employability.careersService: Strong corporate recruitment links similar to Oxford/Cambridge.

notableFacilities: MSB (Mathematical Sciences and Computer Science Building)

personalStatementTips: Durham values academic excellence and engagement
Mention any independent project work
A* in Maths required

cityLife.description: Durham is a small, beautiful UNESCO World Heritage city with a strong collegiate atmosphere and excellent student community.
cityLife.nightlife: Smaller student scene but lively college bars

kings-college-london.name: King's College London
kings-college-london.overview: King's College London is one of the world's top 50 universities, located in the heart of London with campuses on the Strand, Waterloo and Guy's. The Department of Informatics has strong research in AI, robotics and security, with excellent access to London's tech sector.

employability.careersService: King's central London location provides exceptional employer access.

accommodation.description: First-year guaranteed in central London halls.

personalStatementTips: Maths A* required
Show interest in London tech sector

cityLife.description: King's central London location places you at the heart of the capital — Strand campus is opposite Somerset House.

sheffield.name: University of Sheffield
sheffield.overview: The University of Sheffield's Department of Computer Science is one of the largest in the UK and the home of the Verification Anti-Patterns research group, GATE NLP toolkit, and significant AI research. Sheffield's tech and gaming industry (Sumo Digital) provides strong placement opportunities.

employability.careersService: Strong placement programme; Sheffield Tech Cluster growing.

personalStatementTips: Maths required at A or above
Show enthusiasm for CS beyond A-level

cityLife.description: Sheffield is a friendly, affordable student city with access to the Peak District — excellent for outdoor activities.
cityLife.nightlife: Independent music scene; strong student nightlife

st-andrews.name: University of St Andrews
st-andrews.overview: The University of St Andrews is Scotland's oldest university (1413) and one of the UK's most prestigious. The School of Computer Science is small but excellent, with strong research in HCI, security and AI. St Andrews offers a 4-year MA Hons within the Scottish system.

employability.careersService: Strong corporate links; small CS school = personalised support.

facilities.wellbeing: Excellent — small university with personal support

accommodation.privateRentAreas: North Street, South Street, Hope Street

personalStatementTips: St Andrews values genuine intellectual curiosity
Maths A-level required
Show evidence of independent learning

cityLife.description: St Andrews is a small, beautiful coastal town — the home of golf with stunning beaches. Famous for its tight-knit student community.
cityLife.transport: Compact walkable town; bus to Edinburgh

keyFacts: Highest student satisfaction in UK CS
